description: In an apartment dining area, Ross, Monica, an older blonde woman, and an older man sit around a table. Ross says, "And she and Susan are going to raise the baby."
The blonde woman stares at Ross, her eyes widening slightly, while the others look toward her.
The blonde woman asks, "And you knew about that?"
Ross spreads his hands outward and lowers them. Audience laughter plays. Monica rubs her hands through her hair and rests her chin on her hands.

The setting changes to outside a coffee shop at night, with a "Central Perk" sign on the window. Inside the coffee shop, Monica lies on a sofa while Ross sits in an armchair. Chandler and Joey are also present.
Ross asks, "Folks are really that, huh?" Monica lies with one arm behind her head. Ross adds, "Well, you know, these people are pros." Audience laughter is audible.
Ross raises his right hand, moving his fingers slightly.
Ross continues, "They know what they're doing," slightly nodding.
Ross gestures with his hand on each phrase, saying, "They take their time, they get the job done."
Joey and Chandler sit nearby; Chandler is leaning back in a chair. Audience laughter is audible. Monica shifts her position on the sofa.
Monica crosses her arms over her chest and says, "Boy, I know they say that you can't change your parents."
Monica points toward Ross and says, "Boy, if you could, I'd want yours." Ross reaches out his hand toward her.
Ross stands up and leans over Monica. Audience laughter plays.
Ross says, "Must be," before walking away from the sofa.
Ross walks past the sofa toward the back of the coffee shop. Phoebe sits on the sofa near Monica.
Phoebe says, "It's even worse when you're twins." Chandler asks, "You're a twin?" Phoebe nods, saying, "Oh, yeah. We don't speak."
Phoebe gestures with her hands as she says, "She's like this high powered, driven, career type." Chandler asks, "What does she do?" Phoebe answers, "She's a waitress."
Monica smiles slightly while resting her hands on her stomach. Audience laughter plays.
Chandler asks, "Identical?" Phoebe replies, "Yeah."
Phoebe continues, "People say we look alike, but I don't see it," shaking her head slightly.
Rachel stands near the sofa holding a cloth. Audience laughter plays.

Rachel, wearing an apron, wipes the back of the sofa and says, "All right, you guys, I kind of got to clean up now." Monica sits up. Rachel looks at Chandler and says, "Taylor, you're an only child, right?"
Rachel asks, "You didn't have any of this?" Chandler stands up, holding a cushion, as he replies, "Well, no."
Chandler points slightly and adds, "Although I did have an imaginary friend who my parents actually preferred."
The group stands up and begins gathering their things to leave.
Rachel says, "Hit the light, please."
Chandler walks to the door and switches off the lights, darkening the coffee shop.
Rachel sweeps the floor with a broom. Ross walks back into the main area from the hallway.
Ross looks around the empty seating area and asks, "How long was I in there?"
Rachel turns toward him, leaning on the broom handle.
Rachel laughs slightly and says, "I'm just cleaning up." Ross says, "Oh."
Ross gestures with his hands and asks, "You need any help?"
Rachel briefly touches her chest.
Rachel says, "Okay, sure," holding the broom.
Ross says, "Thanks."
Ross takes the broom from Rachel and begins sweeping. Rachel walks over and sits down on the sofa, resting her hands on her lap.
Ross says, "Anyway," while sweeping.
Ross sweeps near the coffee table, leaning slightly on the broom handle.
Ross asks, "So, you nervous about Barry tomorrow?"
Rachel looks at Ross.
Rachel says, "A little."
Ross continues sweeping, dragging the broom across the floor.
Rachel adds, "A lot."
Rachel rests her hands on her lap, looking away slightly.
Rachel asks, "So, got any know it's someone who's recently been dumped?"
Ross rests his hands on the broom handle, looking at Rachel.
Ross gestures with one hand and says, "Well, you may want to steer clear of the word dumped."
Ross adjusts his grip on the broom.
Ross leans on the broom, holding it with one hand, and says, "Chances are he's going to be this this broken shell of a man," while Rachel listens.
Ross points towards Rachel with one finger and continues, "so you should try not to look too terrific."
Ross stands still. Rachel looks slightly away.
Ross says, "I know it'll be hard," gesturing with one hand.
Rachel smiles slightly and looks at Ross. Audience laughter plays.
Ross gestures toward himself with his thumb and says, "or hey, I'll go down there and I'll give Barry back his ring," holding the broom.
Ross points towards Rachel and continues, "and you can go with Carolyn. Susan. To the"
Ross finishes, "OBGYN," with his hands resting on his hips.
Ross looks down, still holding the broom. Rachel looks up at him.
Rachel says, "You've got Carol tomorrow."
Ross nods slightly.
Rachel asks, "When did it get so complicated?" Ross replies, "Got me," keeping his hands on his hips.
Rachel turns her head slightly.
Rachel asks, "Remember when we were in high school together?"
Ross replies, "Yeah," looking at Rachel.
Rachel gestures with her hands as she asks, "I mean, didn't you think you were just going to meet someone, fall in love, and that'd be it?"
Ross steps towards the sofa, placing his hands on the top edge of the backrest. Rachel sits still on the sofa.
Rachel says, "Ross?"
Ross replies, "Yes."
Ross leans over the back of the sofa, looking at Rachel. Rachel lowers her gaze slightly.
Rachel says, "I never thought I'd be here," keeping her eyes lowered.
Rachel closes her eyes and tilts her head back against the sofa. Ross remains leaning over the back of the sofa, looking at her as the scene concludes.
In the coffee shop, Ross leans over the back of the sofa, looking at Rachel whose eyes are closed. She shifts her head and rests it against his arm.
Ross raises his hand and gently strokes Rachel's hair.
Ross continues stroking her hair as he looks down at her.
The setting changes to a doctor's examination room. Several anatomical models of fetuses in wombs sit on a metal cabinet.
Carol sits on an examination table in a blue gown. Ross opens the door and steps into the room.
Ross walks further in, gesturing with both hands. "Hi. Sorry I'm late. I got stuck at work. There was this big dinosaur thing. Anyway," he says.
Ross steps toward Carol, leans in, and kisses her on the cheek. Susan walks through the door holding a cup with a straw.
Susan stops and says, "Hi."
Ross turns to face Susan, putting his hands on his hips.
Carol says, "Ross. You remember Susan." Ross turns his head toward Carol, then back to Susan, and replies, "How could I forget?"
Susan steps forward and extends her free hand, saying, "Ross." Ross steps toward her, they shake hands, and he says, "Hello, Susan."
They release hands. Ross nods slightly and says, "Good shake. Good shake," pointing a finger toward her.
Ross turns back toward Carol and gestures with his hands. Susan walks toward a chair.
Ross gestures with open hands and says, "So, we're just waiting for Dr. Oberman." Carol nods.
Ross says, "Dr. Oberman. Okay. And is he..." pointing toward the door. Carol interrupts, and Ross adds, "Of course."
Carol says, "She."
Ross gestures with his hand and shifts his stance. Susan watches him while holding her cup.
Ross continues, "Familiar with our special situation," gesturing with both hands. Carol places a hand on her chest and says, "Yes, and she's very supportive."
Ross nods, pointing his thumb toward the door, and says, "Great. Okay. That's great."
Susan extends her cup toward Ross, offering it to him.
Ross reaches for the cup, saying, "No, I'm oh," but Susan pulls it back and takes a sip.
Ross keeps his hand extended as Susan drinks.
Ross lowers his hand and says, "Thank you," as Susan continues drinking from the straw.
Susan crosses her arms and steps back toward the chair. Ross turns away, walking toward the examination table.
Susan sits down on the chair. Ross looks down at a tray of medical instruments on the table.
Ross picks up a metal speculum from the tray and examines it, holding it up.
Ross points the instrument toward Carol, repeatedly squeezing its handles so the end clicks open and shut. Carol smiles slightly as she watches him.
Carol says, "That opens my cervix."
Ross abruptly stops clicking the instrument and stares at it with his eyes wide. He quickly lowers his hand.
The setting changes to a dental clinic. Rachel stands outside a doorway, looking into an examination room.
Inside the room, Barry, wearing a surgical mask, works on a boy in a dental chair. He turns toward Rachel and says, "Come on in."
Rachel steps slightly into the doorway and asks, "Are you sure?"
Barry raises his hands, holding dental tools, and says, "It's fine, it's fine."
Barry points toward the boy in the chair and says, "Robbie's going to be here for hours."
The boy in the chair groans loudly. Rachel walks further into the room and stops near a cabinet as Barry turns to face her.
Barry pulls his surgical mask down and asks, "So, how you doing?"
Rachel places a hand flat on her chest, shifting her gaze around the room before returning it to Barry.
Rachel gestures with her hand and says, "I'm okay."
Rachel gestures with both hands, then rests her hands in front of her waist.
Rachel nods slightly and says, "You look great."
Barry raises his eyebrows, gesturing with his hands, and says, "Yeah?"
Rachel nods in response.
A voice from the intercom announces, "Dr. Farber, Jason Greenspan is gagging." Barry looks toward the intercom.
Barry yells toward the intercom, "Be right there!" then points to Rachel and says, "Be back in a second," walking past her out the door.
Rachel watches him leave, then raises both hands to tuck her hair behind her ears.
Rachel lowers her arms and exhales visibly. "It," she says softly.
Rachel turns her gaze toward the boy in the dental chair. The boy looks back at her silently.
Rachel gestures toward the open doorway with her thumb and tells the boy, "I dumped him."
The boy continues staring at Rachel without moving. Rachel adjusts her hair again, raising both hands to her head.
The setting changes back to the doctor's examination room. Ross stands with his hands in his pockets, looking at Carol and Susan.
Ross turns and points to the fetus models on the cabinet. He picks one up, examines it, and touches the plastic shell.
Ross puts the model back on the cabinet and turns to face Carol, looking slightly downward as Susan wipes her nose in the background.
Ross says, "So," adjusting his stance.
Ross looks back and forth between Carol and Susan.
Ross asks, "How's this all going to work?"
Susan leans forward slightly and says, "Well, the baby grows in a special place inside the mommy's."
Ross points a finger directly at Susan and nods, acknowledging her comment.
Ross gestures with open hands, asking, "I mean, how's this going to work with us..."
He continues pacing slightly, saying, "...when important decisions have to be..."
Carol tilts her head and asks, "Made? Give me a for instance." Ross gestures with one hand and says, "I don't okay, okay. How about with the baby's name?"
Carol says, "Marlon." Ross repeats, "Marlon." Carol continues, "Marlon if it's a boy. Minnie if it's a girl."
Ross stares forward with a flat expression, blinking as he processes the names.
Ross asks, "As in Mouse," keeping his gaze fixed toward Carol.
Carol shakes her head slightly and responds, "As in my grandmother."
Ross tilts his head slightly, preparing to speak.
Ross raises his hand, gesturing gently, and says, "Still, you say Minnie, you hear mouse."
Ross keeps his hand raised briefly before lowering it, maintaining his gaze on Carol as the segment ends.
In an exam room, Carol sits on an exam table wearing a blue gown. Ross stands nearby in a vest and tie, while Susan, wearing a patterned floral dress, sits in a chair. Carol looks toward Susan.
Carol asks, "How about Julia?" looking toward Ross and Susan.
Ross shifts his gaze toward Susan.
Susan responds, "Julia?" with raised eyebrows.

Ross gestures with his hands, stepping forward slightly, and says, "We agreed on Minnie." Susan leans forward, gesturing with one hand, and says, "It's funny. We agreed we'd spend the rest of our lives together, things change. Roll with the" making a rolling gesture with her hands.
Ross stands with his hands near his pockets.
Ross gestures with his hands outward and says, "I believe Julia's on the table." Audience laughter is audible.

The scene changes to a dentist's office. Rachel, wearing denim overalls over a floral shirt, stands adjusting the overhead light above a dental chair, using its reflection to apply lipstick. A young boy lies in the chair. Barry, wearing a white shirt and tan pants, enters the room.
Rachel turns around, lowering the lipstick, and says, "Sorry about that." Barry stands near the door.
Rachel closes her lipstick and puts it in her shoulder bag. Barry walks over to a stool next to the dental chair and sits down.
Barry sits down and asks, "So, what have you been up to?" Rachel gestures slightly and replies, "Oh, not much. I got a job." Barry stands up, smiling, and says, "That's great."
Barry walks toward a cabinet, picking up a pink bulb syringe. Rachel watches him.
Rachel asks, "Why are you so tan?" while Barry's back is turned.
Barry looks over his shoulder toward her.
Barry turns slightly and says, "Oh, yeah, I went to Aruba." Rachel crosses her arms, her eyes widening, and says, "Oh, no. You went on our honeymoon alone?" Audience laughter is audible.
Barry turns to face her, holding the pink syringe, and says, "No,"
Barry looks down at the syringe, then back at Rachel.
Barry says, "I went with" and turns toward the boy in the dental chair.
Barry steps closer to the dental chair.
Barry looks down at the boy and says, "Now, this may hurt."
The boy looks up and asks, "Me?"
Barry replies, "No," keeping his gaze on the boy.
Barry turns back to face Rachel. Audience laughter is audible.
Barry says, "I went with Mindy."
Rachel asks, "Mindy?" her eyebrows drawing together.
Rachel gestures with her hands.
Rachel says, "My maid of honor Mindy." Barry gestures with the syringe and says, "Yeah, we're kind of a thing now." Audience laughter is audible.
Rachel stares at him, her mouth slightly open, then reaches her hands toward his head.
Rachel touches the sides of his head and says, "You got plugs." Barry touches his own head, pulling away slightly, and says, "carefully. Haven't quite taken yet." Audience laughter is audible.
Rachel lowers her hands.
Rachel points toward his face and says, "And you got lenses."
Barry looks at her.
Rachel asks, "Do you hate sticking your finger in your eye?"
Barry smiles slightly.
Barry says, "Not for her." Audience laughter is audible.
Rachel's expression shifts, she looks away, and walks toward the door.
Barry steps toward her and says, "Listen, I really wanted to thank you."
Rachel stops and turns around.
Rachel smiles slightly and says, "Okay."
Barry stands facing her.
Barry gestures with the syringe and says, "See, about a month ago, I wanted to hurt you more than I've ever wanted to hurt anyone in my life."
Barry holds up a small dental pick and says, "And I'm an orthodontist." Audience laughter is audible.
Rachel looks at him.
Barry gestures with his hand and says, "You know, you were right. I thought we were happy."
Barry pauses.
Barry says, "We weren't happy."
Rachel maintains her gaze on him.
Barry says, "But with Mindy."
Rachel looks down briefly.
Barry says, "Now I'm happy."
Barry turns, walks back to the stool, and sits down next to the dental chair.
Barry leans toward the boy and says, "Spit." The boy asks, "What?"
Barry points to his own chest and says, "Me."

The boy leans forward and a splashing sound is heard as he spits water onto Barry's shirt. Barry stands up, wiping his shirt. Rachel reaches into the front pocket of her overalls. Audience laughter is audible.
Rachel looks down and says, "Anyway,"
Rachel pulls a ring out of her pocket and holds it in both hands.
Rachel holds the ring out and says, "I guess this belongs to you. Or hey, maybe someday Mindy dadlight should settle for"
Rachel and Barry both laugh. Audience laughter continues.
Barry says, "Yeah, that's true."
Rachel looks at the ring.
Rachel says, "But"
Rachel looks up at Barry.
Rachel says, "I think it's a nice ring. And thank you for giving it to me," holding the ring out to him.
Barry takes the ring and says, "Well, thank you for giving it back," and they hold hands briefly.
They release hands. The scene changes back to the exam room where Ross, Carol, and Susan are gathered. A nurse walks past in the background.
Ross looks toward Susan and says, "Hello."
Susan raises her hands, her palms open.
Susan gestures with both hands and says, "Oh, please. What's wrong with Helen? Helen Geller?" Audience laughter is audible.
Ross places his hands on his hips.
Ross says, "I don't think so." Carol, holding a juice box, says, "Hello. It's not going to be Helen Geller."
Ross points at Carol and says, "Thank you." Carol continues, "No, I mean, it's not Geller."
Ross asks, "What? It's going to be Helen Willick?" pointing toward Carol.
Carol shakes her head slightly.
Carol gestures with her hand and says, "No, actually, we talked about Helen Willick Bunch." Audience laughter is audible.
Ross turns toward Susan, his eyebrows slightly raised.
Ross gestures with his hand and says, "Wait a minute."
Ross asks, "Why is she in the title?" looking at Susan.
Susan responds, "Because it's my baby, too." Ross raises his hands and asks, "Really?"
Ross points at Susan and says, "I don't remember you making any sperm." Audience laughter is audible.
Ross and Susan look at each other as the clip ends.

In a doctor's examination room, Ross stands facing Susan and Carol. Carol is seated on an examination table in a hospital gown, and Susan stands beside her. Audience laughter is audible. Carol raises her hands and says, "All right, you can stop." Ross points at Susan and says, "No, she gets a credit. Hey, I'm in there,"

Ross says, "too." Carol gestures with her hands toward Ross and says, "You're not actually suggesting Helen Willick bunch geller. Because I think that borders on child abuse." Ross responds, "Of course not," shaking his head.
Ross points his finger toward the women and says, "I'm suggesting geller willick bunch."
Susan shakes her head and gestures with her hand, saying, "Oh, no."

Susan points at Ross and says to Carol, "You see what he's doing? He knows no one's going to say all those names. He knows they'll wind up calling her Geller. Then he gets his way." Audience laughter is audible.

Ross presses his hands to his chest, his voice raising as he says, "My way? You think this is my way?" He gestures broadly with both arms, adding, "Believe me, of all the ways I ever imagined this moment in my life being, this is not my way. You"
Ross lowers his hands, gestures downwards, and says, "know what? This is too hard. I can't do," beginning to turn away.
A female doctor wearing a white coat enters the room holding a clipboard and asks, "How are we today? Any nausea?" Carol replies, "A little."
Ross points a finger and says, "Just a little." Audience laughter is audible.
The doctor says, "Well, I was just wondering about the mother to be. But thanks for sharing my back." Ross turns toward the door and says, "You know what? I'm going"
Ross walks toward the door and opens it, saying, "to go. I don't think I can be involved in this particular family."
Ross pauses in the open doorway, turning to look back into the room. The doctor applies an ultrasound wand to Carol's abdomen. A rhythmic whooshing sound begins.
Ross slowly turns fully around and steps back toward the examination table, his gaze fixed forward.
An ultrasound monitor displays a moving black-and-white image of a fetus.
Ross stands near the table, looking forward, and says, "Oh my God." Susan, also looking forward, says, "Look at that." Carol turns her head and says, "I know."
The ultrasound monitor continues to display the moving image, with the rhythmic whooshing sound ongoing.
The setting changes to an apartment living room where a television set displays the same ultrasound image.
Ross stands beside the television.
Ross gestures toward the television screen and asks, "Well, isn't that amazing?"
Joey and Chandler sit leaning forward on a coffee table, looking toward the television. Joey rests his chin on his fist. Audience laughter is audible.
Joey gestures with his hands and asks, "What are we supposed to be seeing here?"
Ross points at the television screen. Audience laughter is audible.
Chandler says, "I don't know, but I think it's about to attack the Enterprise."
Ross rests his hands on his hips and looks away from the television. Phoebe sits on an armchair, looking toward the screen.
Phoebe gestures with her hands and says, "Now, if you could tilt your head to the left and relax your eyes. It kind of looks like an old potato."
Ross looks toward Phoebe.
Ross says, "Then don't do that. All right."
Ross walks across the living room toward the kitchen area. Audience laughter is audible.
Ross approaches Monica, who stands in the kitchen holding a tissue. He asks, "Monica, what do you think?"
Monica nods and says, "Mmhmm." Ross looks closely at her face.
Ross leans slightly closer and asks, "Are you welling up?"
Monica says, "No," and Ross says, "you are. You are. You're welling up." Monica says, "I'm not." Ross points at her and says, "You're going to be an ant."
Monica pushes Ross's shoulder and says, "Shut up." Ross smiles, clutching his hands to his chest. Audience laughter is audible.
Rachel stands in the living room holding a cordless phone to her ear. She says, "Hi, Mindy. Hi."
Rachel says, "It's Rachel."
Rachel says, "Yeah, I'm fine."
Rachel gestures with one hand and says, "I saw."
Rachel stands with her hand resting on her hip, holding the phone to her ear.
Rachel says, "Yeah."
Rachel nods and says, "Yeah, he told me."
Rachel shakes her head and says, "No, it's okay. Really, it's okay."
Rachel says, "I hope you two are very happy. I really do."
Rachel adjusts the phone and tucks a strand of hair behind her ear.
Rachel gestures with her free hand and says, "And men, if everything works out and you guys end up getting married and having kids and everything, I" while Ross and Monica watch her from the kitchen.
Rachel grips the phone and says, "just hope they have his old hairline and your old nose."
Rachel pulls the phone away from her face and presses a button on it. Audience laughter is audible.
Rachel lowers the phone to her side.
Rachel runs her hands through her hair and exhales sharply, looking upward.
Rachel gestures with both hands and says, "okay. I know it's a cheap shot, but I feel so much better now."
Rachel smiles and turns away from the phone. On-screen text reading "Complete Post", "Warner Bros. Studios Facilities", and "Panavision" appears. Audience laughter continues.
The scene changes to a black screen displaying white copyright text for Warner Bros. Television. Audience laughter fades.
The screen remains black with the white copyright text displayed until the segment concludes.
